checklist.claim: the #356 e2e block asserts cheap-gate clean=false AND reason 'workpad status not Complete', non-vacuously
checklist.evidence: block asserted .clean only; reason asserted elsewhere on a synthetic object. Root cause found by orchestrator: the block re-wraps as {signals:.signals}, dropping the top-level reflections field cheap-gate.jq reads.

phase3_findings.description: devflow-review.yml's ENGINE_ERROR guard pin is vacuous: the run.sh pin greps 'ENGINE_ERROR:-false' file-wide, where it also occurs in a prose comment (L1068) and the DERIVED= line (L1164). Deleting the if-guard at L1192 while keeping the flip_review call leaves the pin GREEN while the flip fires on every no-verdict incomplete. Proven by mutation.

The #356 end-to-end cheap-gate assertion re-wraps the bundle as {signals: .signals}, dropping the top-level reflections field the real consumer passes; and asserts only clean=false, which stays GREEN even with the workpad-status check removed (the fixture's reflection bullet independently gates non-clean). Proven by mutation.
flip-review-progress-failed.sh's body guard collapses rc!=0 and rc==0-but-empty into one 'read-failure' breadcrumb.
flip_to_failed()'s interim-guard branch selection is only statically pinned, never executed behaviorally.
The flipped-body cause-line format (italic wrapper, '(run link unavailable)' fallback, multi-line cause collapse) is not pinned.
The shared-final-exit flip passes the bare $DECISION token; reviewer claimed the note reads 'run died: fail'.
Process note: CLAUDE.md hand-edited rather than routed through the claude-md-management skill. Prose verified accurate against HEAD.

flip-review-progress-failed.sh discards the patch failure's stderr (2>&1 to /dev/null), so its breadcrumb cannot distinguish a transient blip from a token-scope error — the distinction the file's own comment (L86-92) argues is load-bearing for id/body.

fix_decisions.claim_text: body guard collapses rc!=0 and rc==0-but-empty
fix_decisions.evidence: Suggestion severity, below the resolved fix_severity_threshold 'important'; diagnostic-only impact (breadcrumb wording), no behavioral fail-direction.
Suggestion (criticality 3); inline-workflow-bash-is-static-pinned is this repo's established convention, and the guard's absence-from-resume-arm invariant IS pinned.
Suggestion (criticality 2); diagnostic-only, a regression degrades a breadcrumb rather than behavior.

fix_decisions.claim_text: the workpad note reads 'run died: fail — <url>' for the cap-exhausted arm
fix_decisions.evidence: False. $DECISION at that site holds a token from stall-backstop-decide.sh's vocabulary; on the cap-exhausted arm it is 'fail-exhausted', so the note reads 'run died: fail-exhausted — <url>', which is already descriptive. The reviewer's quoted string 'fail' does not occur.

fix_decisions.claim_text: CLAUDE.md hand-edited without the claude-md-management skill
fix_decisions.evidence: Process observation, not a code defect; the reviewer itself verified the added prose is accurate against HEAD. Out of scope for a fix-loop commit.
Suggestion severity; the patch arm still emits a distinct, arm-naming breadcrumb and the helper still exits 0. Asymmetry with id/body is real but diagnostic-only.

Re-affirmed at Important. Observable fail-direction: the pin fails OPEN — a mutation deleting the guard leaves the suite GREEN (empirically demonstrated), so the suite does NOT catch it RED. This is not a fail-closed or diagnostic-only defect; a real behavioral regression (flip firing on every no-verdict incomplete) would ship undetected. No over-grade shape matches.

fix_decisions.claim_text: e2e cheap-gate assert is vacuous and feeds a shape the producer never emits
fix_decisions.evidence: Re-affirmed at Important. Observable fail-direction: the assertion fails OPEN — with the workpad-status check neutralized the clean-only assert stays GREEN (empirically demonstrated). The test also exercises a bundle shape the producer never emits, so cheap-gate's reflection check was inert. Not suite-RED, not diagnostic-only.

phase3_findings.description: flip_to_failed's inline bash in devflow-implement.yml has no behavioral test; only static grep pins.
The steps.engine is_error producer wiring in devflow.yml is only presence-pinned; the fail-safe (absent/unparseable log -> false) is not asserted at this integration point.
The patch-failure arm discards the underlying gh cause (2>&1 to /dev/null), unlike the id/body arms in the same helper.
The `grep -q "💥')"` source pin on _STATUS_GLYPHS is textually fragile to a tuple reflow.
devflow.yml's engine-parse and flip steps run for every comment-triggered command, incurring a no-op workpad.py id round-trip on non-review triggers.

evaluations.evidence: Shape 3 (uncorroborated single-source from pr-test-analyzer, an empirical over-grader; no other Phase-3 agent raised it and no Phase-2 FAIL covers it). Observable fail-direction: the guard `[ "${CLASS:-}" = "interim" ] || return 0` fails CLOSED when CLASS is unset or wrong — it returns 0 and performs NO flip. The highest-value invariant (absence from the resume) arm) IS pinned with a positive control. Inline workflow bash is static-pinned by repo convention; the agent itself calls the gap convention-consistent.

Shape 1 (fail-closed). A false-positive is_error cannot flip a healthy review comment: flip-review-progress-failed.sh:151 flips only a 🚀-prefixed Status, and a healthy completed review has already written its verdict, so the helper takes the TERMINAL no-op arm. parse-engine-error.sh's own fail-safe (absent/unparseable -> false) carries pre-existing tests. Bounded, fail-closed blast radius, not silent corruption.

evaluations.finding: patch-failure arm discards the gh cause
evaluations.evidence: Shape 2 (diagnostic-only, no behavioral fail-direction). Both branches of the patch `if` emit stderr only; the helper still exits 0 and the Status is left unchanged either way. The sole observable impact is the wording of a breadcrumb. Real and worth fixing, but not an Important blast radius. It is NOT a diff-added doc/comment falsehood, so the shape-2 truthfulness exclusion does not apply.
